title: Tutorial: Accessing and Exploring the Data
text: [Tutorial intro goes here — e.g. “This tutorial walks through fetching, cleaning, and doing initial exploration of the Utah housing affordability dataset.”]

title: Tutorial: Accessing and Exploring the Data
text:
Installation
Install the utah-housing package and set your Census API key before running any code cells.
pip install utah-housing
Set your Census API key (obtain a free key at https://api.census.gov/data/key_signup.html):

import os
os.environ["CENSUS_API_KEY"] = "your_census_api_key_here"

title: Tutorial: Accessing and Exploring the Data
text:
Reading in the Data

import pandas as pd
from utah_housing import fetch_all_years, OUTCOME, BASE_PREDICTORS, COMPLEX_PREDICTORS

# Fetch ACS 5-year estimates for Utah census tracts, 2009–2023
df = fetch_all_years(years=range(2009, 2024))
df.head()


# Save to CSV so you can reload without hitting the API again
df.to_csv("data/utah_housing.csv", index=False)


# Reload from CSV (faster than re-fetching)
df = pd.read_csv("data/utah_housing.csv")
df.head()

title: Tutorial: Accessing and Exploring the Data
text:
Initial Exploration

print("Shape:", df.shape)
print("\nOutcome variable:", OUTCOME)
print("Base predictors:", BASE_PREDICTORS)
print("Complex predictors:", COMPLEX_PREDICTORS)


# Check for missing values in key columns
analysis_cols = ["year", "GEOID"] + [OUTCOME] + COMPLEX_PREDICTORS
df[analysis_cols].isnull().sum()

title: Tutorial: Accessing and Exploring the Data
text:
Cleaning the Data


# Drop rows missing the outcome variable
df_clean = df.dropna(subset=[OUTCOME]).copy()

# Extract county name from the NAME field for easier filtering
df_clean["county_name"] = df_clean["NAME"].str.extract(r",\s*(.+?)\s+County", expand=False)

print(f"Rows after cleaning: {len(df_clean):,}")
df_clean.head()

title: Tutorial: Accessing and Exploring the Data
text:
Subsetting by County or Year

# Filter to a specific county
salt_lake = df_clean[df_clean["county_name"] == "Salt Lake"]
print(salt_lake.shape)

# Filter to a specific year
df_2023 = df_clean[df_clean["year"] == 2023]
print(df_2023.shape)

title: Tutorial: Accessing and Exploring the Data
text:
Basic Summary Statistics

summary_cols = [OUTCOME] + COMPLEX_PREDICTORS
df_clean[summary_cols].describe().round(2)



The data is now ready for EDA and modeling (see the Technical Report).

What’s Inside

Documentation — Data source, variables, and the utah-housing Python package.
Tutorial — Step-by-step guide to fetching, cleaning, and exploring the data.
Technical Report — EDA, modeling, and findings.
Interactive App — Explore the data live with the Streamlit app.

title: Utah Housing Data Acquisition
text:
ACS 5-year estimates for Utah census tracts, 2009–2023.
Run this notebook once to fetch the data and save it to data/utah_housing.csv. After that, the CSV can be reloaded directly without hitting the Census API again.

title: Utah Housing Data Acquisition
text:
1. Setup

import os
import pandas as pd
from utah_housing import fetch_all_years, OUTCOME, BASE_PREDICTORS, COMPLEX_PREDICTORS

title: Utah Housing Data Acquisition
text:
2. Census API Key
Obtain a free key at: https://api.census.gov/data/key_signup.html
Set it below, or store it in a .env file and load with python-dotenv.

# TODO: Replace with your Census API key (do NOT commit this to git)
os.environ["CENSUS_API_KEY"] = "your_census_api_key_here"

title: Utah Housing Data Acquisition
text:
3. Data Acquisition
Fetch ACS 5-year estimates for all Utah census tracts across 2009–2023.

df = fetch_all_years(years=range(2009, 2024))
print(f"Total rows: {len(df):,}")
df.head()

title: Utah Housing Data Acquisition
text:
4. Save Data

os.makedirs("data", exist_ok=True)
df.to_csv("data/utah_housing.csv", index=False)
print("Saved to data/utah_housing.csv")

title: Utah Housing Data Acquisition
text:
5. Initial Exploration

df.head()


print("Shape:", df.shape)
print("\nOutcome variable:", OUTCOME)
print("Base predictors:", BASE_PREDICTORS)
print("Complex predictors:", COMPLEX_PREDICTORS)


df.dtypes


df.describe()


# Check missing values in key analysis columns
analysis_cols = ["year", "GEOID"] + [OUTCOME] + COMPLEX_PREDICTORS
df[analysis_cols].isnull().sum()

title: Documentation
text:
Python Package: utah-housing

The utah-housing package provides functions to fetch ACS data directly from the Census API and compute derived housing affordability metrics.

Installation
pip install utah-housing

Note: A free Census API key is required. Obtain one at https://api.census.gov/data/key_signup.html and set it as an environment variable:
export CENSUS_API_KEY="your_key_here"



Core Functions

from utah_housing import fetch_all_years, OUTCOME, BASE_PREDICTORS, COMPLEX_PREDICTORS

# Fetch ACS 5-year estimates for all years
df = fetch_all_years(years=range(2009, 2024))

# Inspect variable groupings
print("Outcome:", OUTCOME)
print("Base predictors:", BASE_PREDICTORS)
print("Complex predictors:", COMPLEX_PREDICTORS)



Function Reference








Function
Description




fetch_all_years(years)
Fetches ACS 5-year estimates for all Utah census tracts across the given year range


OUTCOME
String constant naming the outcome variable


BASE_PREDICTORS
List of basic predictor variable names


COMPLEX_PREDICTORS
List of extended predictor variable names
